title: Check a recorded history for linearizability
description: Feed a recorded operation history into a consistency checker and report any anomaly with the offending operations.
body: Use when you have a recorded concurrent history and need to know whether it is linearizable (or satisfies a weaker target model). Actions: detect the available checker (Knossos/Porcupine or the project's harness), supply the history and the model specification, run it, and report the verdict plus the minimal anomalous operation sequence if it fails. Do not declare a system linearizable from a passing run that included no concurrency or faults. This skill exposes capability only and must not run against production data without runtime permission. Validation signals include a checker pass over histories that include partitions and concurrent clients. Risk boundary: an anomaly means an acked guarantee is broken; surface it as a blocking finding and recommend escalation rather than re-running until it passes.
